c.d: The maximum size of a single file that can be transferred using the app.
The average speed at which files are transferred between devices using the app.
The range of operating systems and devices supported by the app (e.g., Windows, macOS, Android, iOS).
The security measures implemented by the app, such as end-to-end encryption, password protection, and compliance with data protection regulations.

c.n: User Interface and Ease of Use
c.d: The intuitiveness and user-friendliness of the app's interface, including ease of navigation and simplicity of the file transfer process.
The pricing structure of the app, including availability of free versions, subscription tiers, and any associated costs.
The app's ability to integrate with other platforms and services, such as cloud storage providers, email clients, and productivity tools.
The app's capability to transfer files without an active internet connection, using methods like Wi-Fi Direct or Bluetooth.
The variety of file types and formats that can be transferred using the app, including documents, images, videos, and executables.
The underlying technology or protocol used by the app to transfer files, such as peer-to-peer, cloud-based, or Wi-Fi Direct.
